A 2023, 874, 145007. ![](images/6fc5f56a65be3073779805e7a8acfc80ea5cc1a11d84f8f24854aad2fc9be6fa.jpg) <details> <summary>natural_image</summary> Portrait photo of a young man with short brown hair wearing a checkered shirt (no text or symbols visible) </details> Valentyn Veis, Ph.D., is a researcher in the Department of Special Steels and Alloys at the Physico-Technological Institute of Metals and Alloys of the NAS of Ukraine. His fundamental studies focus on crystallization processes and structure formation in carbon and alloy steel castings. He investigates the influence of temperature kinetic parameters on the structure and properties of steel castings and methods to enhance their properties using hereditary modification and temperature–time melt processing during precrystallization, crystallization, and solid-phase transformation stages. ![](images/ed5abbf4acddf8e28e0b32466edb782f9ba01c9a3ae55f08ad1c9421497903a7.jpg) <details> <summary>natural_image</summary> Portrait of a woman with blonde hair tied up in a bun, wearing earrings (no text or symbols visible) </details> Anastasiia Semenko, Ph.D., is a senior researcher in the MHD Department at the Physico-Technological Institute of Metals and Alloys of the NAS of Ukraine (Kyiv). Her current research interests include advanced steels with high strength, development of numerical models, and simulation of solidification and multiphase phenomena during metal casting. ![](images/ef0eceb37cb8f7ea8ddcad10a368022b34d0b5f49a82cf9ab624e78d1fac844c.jpg) <details> <summary>natural_image</summary> Portrait of a man with beard wearing suit and tie, against a blue abstract background (no text or symbols) </details> Mykhailo Voron, Ph.D., is a senior research scientist at the Physico-Technological Institute of Metals and Alloys of the NAS of Ukraine. He graduated from the National Technical University of Ukraine “Igor Sikorsky Kyiv Polytechnic Institute.” He has been conducting research in materials science, foundry, and metallurgy since 2007. His interests include twin-induced plasticity mechanism steels, creep-resistant and special Al alloys, Ti alloy casting, biocompatible Ti-based alloys, materials design, metal matrix composites, additive manufacturing, and recycling. He focuses on materials for future applications with higher strength, lower weight, and enhanced operational properties. ![](images/8a22038ad35ed57d6e01dcd5dc110aea6d17afb41ef4f812991247e5d7884c84.jpg) <details> <summary>natural_image</summary> Portrait of a man in formal suit and tie (no visible text or symbols) </details> Andrii Tymoshenko, Ph.D., is a senior researcher at the Department of Concentrated Energy Impacts, Physico-Technological Institute of Metals and Alloys of the NAS of Ukraine. He is engaged in the creation of new iron–carbon and high-manganese Fe–Mn–Al–C alloys with improved physical, mechanical, and operational properties by optimizing chemical composition and heat treatment. He also develops innovative methods for producing bimetallic and composite materials using concentrated energy sources such as laser, induction heating, and their combinations. ![](images/26f0e3789aa218da79b0d1f8168dc0f4d415f83eced24a63fbc1fb5ea7d13aea.jpg) <details> <summary>natural_image</summary> Portrait of a man with short dark hair and beard, wearing a black shirt (no text or symbols visible) </details> Richard Likhatskyi, PhD is a Research Scientist at the Physico-Technological Institute of Metals and Alloys of the NAS of Ukraine. He graduated from the National Technical University of Ukraine “Igor Sikorsky Kyiv Polytechnic Institute”. He has been involved in research on materials design, foundry, and electrometallurgy since 2019. His fields of interest include materials science, metal-matrix composites, TWIP steels, creep-resistant copper-based alloys, electron-beam foundry technology, and computer modelling of casting processes. He focuses on developing low-alloyed materials with enhanced operational and physical properties. ![](images/666f80f05377deeaf9439a3ebd893bd7cbff9070be97080e0694a5e93f708883.jpg) <details> <summary>natural_image</summary> Portrait of a man with short dark hair wearing a black shirt (no text or symbols visible) </details> Ivan Likhatskyi is a postgraduate student at the Physico-Technological Institute of Metals and Alloys of the NAS of Ukraine. He graduated from the National Technical University of Ukraine “Igor Sikorsky Kyiv Polytechnic Institute.” He has been conducting research in materials design, foundry, and electrometallurgy since 2020. His fields of interest include materials science, twin-induced plasticity mechanism steels, creep-resistant and high-entropy alloys, high-performance multicomponent alloys, electron-beam foundry technology, and electrometallurgy. He focuses on experimental work on new alloys and high-alloyed materials with enhanced operational properties. ![](images/4404b945168a9d89eb7da8c5c3ae76354512b0d07c31187c8d5f6ad40874ceec.jpg) <details> <summary>natural_image</summary> Portrait of a woman with short brown hair wearing a striped shirt and blue top (no text or symbols visible) </details> Zhanna Parkhomchuk is a senior researcher in the Department of Special Steels and Alloys at the Physico-Technological Institute of Metals and Alloys of the NAS of Ukraine. Her main research areas include fundamental studies on the control of crystallization and structure formation in cast structures. She develops new technological processes using external influences and controlled heat removal during crystallization and solid-phase transformations of steel castings. She is the author of 70 scientific publications.
